evidence: The paper explicitly states: "The ranking is based on permutation feature importance" (Table 1 caption). Methods section confirms: "we used permutation strategies to determine the unique contribution of the respective behavioral class...These effects were also tested for significance (41) and adjusted for multiple comparisons" (ref. 41: Altmann et al. 2010 permutation importance). The paper ranks behavioral classes: "communication and social behavior had the biggest impact on model performance on domain (β = 0.027, CI 95% = [.026, .028]) and facet levels." However, permutation importance is robust to the impurity-bias mechanism; the central conclusion is personality predictability, not the specific ranking of behavioral drivers.
